primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer si la juste valeur doit intégrer le risque de non-exécution ou de défaut dans une perspective de marché, et comment cette logique se décline pour un actif par rapport à un passif.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule by selecting IFRS 13 as the single governing lineage because it directly defines fair value and sets the measurement framework for assets and liabilities, while no lower-precedence overlapping governing document in the provided context supersedes or competes with it on this core issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low — the question is directly about the market-based content of fair value, which is expressly governed by IFRS 13.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern fair value as a market-based exit price and require market participant risk assumptions, including risk premiums and fulfilment-risk concepts for liabilities.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs support the analysis by illustrating how fair value and liability credit risk are treated within IFRS 9, especially for liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss.

peripheral_authority.reason: This standard requires retirement benefit plan investments to be carried at fair value but does not help identify the peer fair value approaches for counterparty or non-performance risk.
This standard addresses policy selection and estimation uncertainty generally, not the substantive fair value risk content of assets and liabilities.
This interpretation uses fair value in measuring a dividend payable but does not contribute to identifying the relevant peer fair value models for this issue.

treatment_families.family: Market-based fair value measurement of an asset using market participant assumptions and risk-adjusted pricing inputs
Market-based fair value measurement of a liability incorporating fulfilment and non-performance risk through market participant assumptions

approaches.label: Fair value of an asset
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 13 defines a distinct fair value measurement perspective for assets based on the price to sell, using market participant assumptions and risk-adjusted inputs.

approaches.label: Fair value of a liability
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 13 defines a distinct fair value measurement perspective for liabilities based on the price to transfer, including market participant assumptions about fulfilment and non-performance risk.
